name: resfo

metadata.name: resfo
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: A (lazy) parser and writer for reservoir simulator fortran output format.

documentation:
resfo
===========
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/resfo/badge/?version=latest&style=plastic)](https://resfo.readthedocs.io/)

resfo (Reservoir simulator fortran output) is a parser for the output format
used by several reservoir simulators such as [opm
flow](https://github.com/OPM/opm-simulators), such as found in files with
extensions .UNRST, .EGRID, .INIT, etc. and also the corresponding ascii files
with extension .FUNRST, .FEGRID, .FINIT, etc.


Installation
============

resfo can be installed with pip:

```bash
pip install resfo
```

Getting started
===============

Reservoir simulator output files consist of a sequence of named arrays. resfo
does not interpret the names, but simply give you a tuple of the name and a
numpy array with the read function:

```
import resfo

for kw, arr in resfo.read("my_grid.egrid"):
    print(kw)

>>> "FILEHEAD"
>>> "GRIDHEAD"
>>> "COORD"
>>> "ZCORN"
>>> "ACTNUM"
>>> "MAPAXES"
```

For more information, see [the docs](http://resfo.rtfd.io).


How to contribute
=================

The easiest way to set up a nice development environment for resfo is to use tox (can be installed via `pip install tox` or
`sudo apt install tox` on ubuntu), then all testing and linting can be ran with

```bash
tox
```

You can also set up `pre-commit` to ensure style checks are done as you commit:

```bash
pip install pre-commit
pre-commit install
```

functions.name: resfo.write.write
documentation:
Write the given contents to the given file in res format.

```
param filelike

Either filename, pathlib.Path or stream
to write file to. For fileformat=Format.UNFORMATTED the
stream must be in binary mode and for fileformat=Format.FORMATTED
in text mode.

param contents

list or iterable of tuples (kw, arr) where keyword
is the keyword, and arr is a numpy arraylike of values. The
keyword must have exactly 8 characters and the type of the array
will be converted according to resfo.types.to_np_type

param fileformat

Either resfo.Format.FORMATTED for ascii
format or resfo.Format.UNFORMATTED for binary format.

raises resfo.ResfoWriteError

If the given contents cannot be
written to an res file.
```

:::{note}
If given a file to be open (as opposed to a stream), the errors
(various `IOError` s) associated with the default behavior of the
built-in `open()` function may be raised.

When given a stream, the exceptions associated with the stream will
pass through.
:::

classes.name: resfo.errors.ResfoParsingError
documentation: Indicates an error occurred during reading of an res file.

classes.name: resfo.errors.ResfoWriteError
documentation: Indicates an error occurred during writing of an res file.

classes.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.__init__
documentation:
---
param stream: |-
  The opened res file with stream.peek() at
  the start of the res array.
---

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.is_eof
documentation:
Whether the position of the array is actually
at the end of the file, in which case its keyword
and array is None.

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.read_keyword
documentation:
Read the keyword from the res file.

:returns: The keyword as a 8 character string.

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.read_length
documentation:
Read the length from the res file.

:returns: The length of the array in number of entries.

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.read_array
documentation:
Read the array from the unformatted res file.

:returns: numpy array of values.

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.read_type
documentation:
The type given in the header of the array.
In case of unformatted file this will be bytes, for
unformatted files it is str.

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.update
documentation:
Updates the entry with the given new data.

```
param new_keyword

The new keyword, must be 8 characters, defaults to no change.

param new_array

The new array, must be of same type and size
as existing array, defaults to no change.

raises ValueError

If array does not have the same type or length as
existing array.

raises io.UnsupportedOperation

If the underlying stream has been opened
with the wrong mode (file must be opened with "r+" in order to correctly update)
```

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray._read
documentation:
Reads the array entry. Guarantees that after, either entry.is_eof() and
the stream.peek() is at the end of the file or stream.peek() will be at
the start of the next record.

methods.name: resfo.array_entry.ResArray.parse
documentation:
Parse an res file from the given opened file handle.

Is a generator of ResArrays

An array entry in a res file.

This class is not ment to be constructed directly, but rather
generated e.g. {py:meth}`resfo.lazy_read`.

functions.name: resfo.format.guess_format
documentation:
Guess the format of a given filelike.

If given a filename or pathlib.Path the guess is based on
seeing if the first 4 bytes are a fortran marker. If given
a stream, assumes byte stream means Format.FORMATTED and
text stream means Format.UNFORMATTED.

```
param filelike

Either a filename, pathlib.Path or opened stream.

returns

Either Format.FORMATTED or Format.UNFORMATTED.
```

functions.name: resfo.format.get_stream
documentation:
Openes the given file with the correct mode (text or binary)
based on fileformat.
\:param filepath: Either a filename or pathlib.Path
\:param fileformat: A resfo.Format.
\:returns: The opened file.

functions.name: resfo.format.check_correct_mode
documentation:
Checks that the stream is the correct mode (text or binary) for the given
fileformat.

classes.name: resfo.format.Format
documentation:
The format of an res file, either FORMATTED for ascii
or UNFORMATTED for binary.

:returns: The number of bytes for each element in an

documentation:
---
param array_length: Number of items in the array
param item_type: Type of items in the array
returns: |-

modules.name: resfo._unformatted
documentation:
This module implements the read and write
functionality for unformatted res files
(see {ref}`unformatted-format`) and is not
part of the resfo public API.

documentation:
Read the array from the unformatted res file.
:returns: numpy array of values.

methods.name: resfo._unformatted.read.UnformattedResArray._read_record_marker
documentation:
Read 4 bytes and check that it matches the
expected fortan header/footer

methods.name: resfo._unformatted.read.UnformattedResArray._read_keyword
documentation:
With stream.peek() at the start of the keyword, reads
it into self._keyword

methods.name: resfo._unformatted.read.UnformattedResArray._read_type
documentation:
With stream.peek() at the start of the type string, reads

documentation: With stream.peek() at the start of the length, reads it and returns it.

methods.name: resfo._unformatted.read.UnformattedResArray._read
documentation: See resfo.array_entry.ResArray._read()
An array entry in a unformatted res file.

functions.name: resfo.read.read
documentation:
Read the contents of a res file and return a list of
tuples (keyword, array). Takes the same parameters as
lazy_read, but differs in return type

functions.name: resfo.read.lazy_read
documentation:
Reads the contents of an res file and generates the entries
of that file. Each entry has a entry.read_keyword() and
entry.read_array() method which will return the corresponding
data, but only upon request. This requires the user to
pay attention to when values are read as it should happen
before the file is closed.

When lazy_read is given a path or filename, the file will be closed once
the generator has ran out of elements.

For greater control, one can pass an opened file so that close
can be called at the correct time.

```
param filelike

Either filename, pathlib.Path or stream
to write file to. For fileformat=Format.UNFORMATTED the
stream must be in binary mode and for fileformat=Format.FORMATTED
in text mode.

param fileformat

Either resfo.Format.FORMATTED for ascii
format, resfo.Format.UNFORMATTED for binary formatted files
or None for guess.

raises resfo.ResfoParsingError

If the file is not a valid
res file.
```

:::{note}
If given a file to be open (as opposed to a stream), the errors
(various `IOError` s) associated with the default behavior of the
built-in `open()` function may be raised.

When given a stream, the exceptions associated with the stream will
pass through.
:::

modules.name: resfo.types
documentation:
The types of elements in the res output files are
INTE for 32-bit integers, REAL for 32-bit floats,
LOGI for 32-bit fortran logicals, DOUB for 64-bit floats,
CHAR for 8 byte ascii strings, and C0nn for nn byte ascii strings.

All numerical types are big endian.

Additionally there are two types that signal something other than
what is contained in the arrays. X231 means that the following array
has more than 2**31 elements so the length is split into two headers.

The other is MESS type which signals a 0 length array, so the type
of elements is irrelevant.


A res type is represented by a byte string of ascii characters
having one of the above mentioned keywords.

resfo converts res types to corresponding numpy dtypes
and back. Some numpy dtypes will give a loss of precision and
that results in a warning.

functions.name: resfo.types.to_np_type
documentation:
---
param type_keyword: A bytestring of a res type.
returns: |-
  A np dtype corresponding to the given
  res type.
---

functions.name: resfo.types.from_np_dtype
documentation:
---
param array: A numpy array
returns: |-
  The corresponding res type for the
  given numpy array's dtype.
---

functions.name: resfo.types.is_valid_type
documentation: :returns: Whether the given byte string is a valid res type.

functions.name: resfo.types.is_character_type
documentation:
:returns: Whether the given byte string as ascii is
    a string res type, ie. b"CHAR" or b"C016".

classes.name: resfo.types.MESS
documentation:
The MESS value is a sentinell object used to signal that the type of the
array that is to be written should be an empty array of type MESS.

modules.name: resfo._formatted
documentation:
This module implements the read and write
functionality for formatted res files (see

> {ref}`formatted-format`) and is

not part of the resfo public API.

functions.name: resfo._formatted.write.write_str_list
documentation:
Writes the given list of strings to the res file.
The strings will have the length of the longest string,
left-padded with space and enclosed by single-quotes.

functions.name: resfo._formatted.write.write_np_array
documentation:
Writes the given numpy array to the stream as
the given res_type.
\:param stream: stream to write to.
\:param array: Numpy array.
\:param res_type: The res type to use for the array, see

> resfo.types.

functions.name: resfo._formatted.write.write_entry
documentation:
Write the given array/keyword entry to the
stream in the formatted res file format.
\:param stream: text-mode stream to write to.
\:param keyword: 8-character string to use for keyword.
\:param array_like: Array of values to write.

functions.name: resfo._formatted.write.formatted_write
documentation:
Writes the list of data entries to the stream as
formatted res.
\:param stream: File handle in text mode.
\:param keyworded_arrays: Either iterable or list of (kw, array)

> tuples for entries to be written to the file. Also takes dictionary
> of keyword: array pairs.

functions.name: resfo._formatted.read.drop_while_space
documentation:
Forwards the stream to the first non-space character.
Assumes the stream is in text mode.

classes.name: resfo._formatted.read.FormattedArray

methods.name: resfo._formatted.read.FormattedArray._read_logical
documentation:
Read one fortran logical ('T' or 'F') from
the beginning of the stream and returns it.

methods.name: resfo._formatted.read.FormattedArray._read_quote_separated
documentation:
Read one single-quote delimited string from the
start of the stream, and returns it.

methods.name: resfo._formatted.read.FormattedArray._read_number
documentation: Read one number from the stream and returns it as a string.

methods.name: resfo._formatted.read.FormattedArray._read
documentation: See resfo.array_entry.ResArray._read()
An array entry in an formatted res file.
